hypothesis:
Based on the finding that current general-purpose metrics (validity, novelty, uniqueness, diversity, FCD) fail to correlate with domain-specific material performance (percentage passing expert filters), I propose:

**Hypothesis:** Incorporating differentiable approximations of material-specific structural constraints (symmetry, flatness, conjugation, steric hindrance) directly into the generative model's loss function during pre-training—rather than only as post-hoc reward signals during reinforcement learning—will enable more efficient transfer learning to materials discovery tasks while reducing the computational cost and policy collapse risk associated with RL-based approaches.
